skill.name: update-docs
skill.description: Updates project documentation such as feature specifications, database design notes, error code lists, and README files.
skill.summary: Keep project documents current while preserving their existing Markdown structure.
skill.author: Crearize

security_audit.summary: The high static findings are false positives caused by Markdown front matter, inline code paths, and file names ending in .md. One shell example uses cat with a placeholder document path, which is a legitimate documentation workflow but should stay limited to project documentation files.

low_findings.title: Shell Read Command Uses Placeholder Path
low_findings.description: The skill shows a bash example that runs cat against [document_path]. This is normal for a documentation update skill, but the path should remain within intended project documentation files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command is visible in a Markdown bash block and reads a placeholder path. It has legitimate documentation use, but unrestricted paths could expose unintended local files.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backticks Misclassified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: Inline Markdown paths and a completion report template were flagged as Ruby or shell backtick execution. These lines are documentation text, not executable Ruby code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are Markdown inline code or a Markdown fenced block marker. No Ruby execution context or executable script is present.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Detection Is a File Extension False Positive
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography findings appear to match Markdown text or .md file paths. No cryptographic algorithm, hash function call, or security-sensitive operation is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines contain a description or Markdown document paths. I found no evidence of MD5, SHA1, or other weak cryptographic usage.

low_findings.title: Entropy Heuristic Is a Readable Markdown False Positive
low_findings.description: The entropy warning points to the Markdown file header, but the file is plain text documentation. No binary, encrypted payload, or obfuscated content was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The entire reviewed file is readable Markdown with Japanese text and simple examples. I found no evidence of encoded payloads or hidden executable content.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Command Example
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes a bash example using cat [document_path]. This is low risk when restricted to known project documentation files.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command exists, but it is a read-only example for documentation review. No network transfer or destructive command is present.

content.value_statement: Project documents often fall behind implementation changes. This skill guides updates to specifications, database notes, error code lists, and README files.
actual_capabilities: Identifies the target document type before making updates.
Supports feature specifications, database design documents, error code lists, and README files.
Reads the existing document before applying requested changes.
Preserves the current Markdown structure during edits.
Provides a completion report with the updated type, path, and change summary.
limitations: It only describes workflows for Markdown-based project documentation.
It does not define validation rules for every project documentation format.
It relies on the user to provide accurate update content.
It should not read files outside the intended project documentation scope.

use_cases.title: Refresh Feature Specifications
use_cases.description: Update a feature specification after requirements or behavior changes.

use_cases.title: Maintain Architecture Notes
use_cases.description: Keep database design documentation aligned with schema changes.

use_cases.title: Improve Project Onboarding
use_cases.description: Revise README content so setup and usage instructions stay current.

prompt_templates.title: Update a README
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a common documentation update.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the update-docs skill to update the README with these setup changes: [describe the changes].

prompt_templates.title: Revise a Feature Spec
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request for a feature document.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the update-docs skill with document_type="feature" for [feature name]. Preserve the current structure and add these behavior changes: [details].

prompt_templates.title: Update Database Documentation
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the update-docs skill with document_type="database". Update the database design notes for these schema changes: [tables, fields, relationships].

prompt_templates.title: Coordinate Multiple Doc Changes
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the update-docs skill to update the feature spec, error code list, and README for this release. Keep terminology consistent: [release details].

output_examples.input: Update the README with a new environment variable and a revised install step.
output_examples.output: The README was updated with the requested setup details. The completion report lists the README path and summarizes the environment and install changes.

output_examples.input: Update the error code list for two new validation failures.
output_examples.output: The error code documentation was revised while keeping its existing Markdown structure. The completion report identifies the updated document and added entries.

best_practices: Provide the document type, target path, and exact changes in one request.
Review the existing document before asking for major rewrites.
Keep update content specific so the final report is easy to verify.
anti_patterns: Do not use the skill to inspect unrelated local files.
Do not request broad rewrites without clear source changes.
Do not mix unrelated feature, database, and README updates without context.

faq.question: What types of documents does this skill support?
faq.answer: It supports feature specifications, database design documents, error code lists, and README files.

faq.question: Does it preserve Markdown formatting?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill instructs the assistant to preserve the existing Markdown format and structure.

faq.question: Can it update files outside the documents directory?
faq.answer: It can update README files, but document reads should stay within intended project documentation paths.

faq.question: Does it generate a completion report?
faq.answer: Yes. It reports the document type, path, updated content, and next review step.

faq.question: Is this skill safe for marketplace use?
faq.answer: The audit found low risk. The only real concern is a read-only shell example using a placeholder path.

faq.question: Does it support Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The report marks the skill as compatible with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
